weak_cycles.issue: Re-adds a 'thin line of moonlight on still water' SVG that already exists from cycle 0 with nearly identical markup — duplicates the durable anchor instead of letting it hold.
weak_cycles.suggestion: Drop the redundant addSVG and only place the 'alone in the room' text; trust the cycle-0 anchor to breathe.

weak_cycles.issue: fadeElement targets 'elem_0002' which is the cycle-2 solitary silhouette image, not the 'early opening text' the plan calls to retire. This contradicts the stated retire_at for the opening text and prematurely loses a planted figure.
weak_cycles.suggestion: Fade the cycle-1 text element instead (the 'alone in the room' fragment), preserving the silhouette anchor.

weak_cycles.issue: fadeElement again targets 'elem_0002' (already faded at cycle 10) — a no-op that mis-tracks IDs and fails to actually retire the warm bloom / pivot composite as the plan specifies.
weak_cycles.suggestion: Fade the cycle-7 composite group (group_0007) here per plan retire_at, or the warm bloom SVG specifically; verify IDs against actual element registry.

weak_cycles.issue: Tries to fade 'group_0013' but that group is the muhayyar arrival composite which is supposed to remain dominant through cycle 16 and only fully retire later; also paletteShift duplicates the cycle-16 shift with identical params.
weak_cycles.suggestion: Fade group_0007 (first pivot composite, due to retire at 16) and let group_0013 linger; skip the redundant paletteShift or use a different target.

weak_cycles.issue: Empty tool_calls but rationale claims 'feather settles via transformElement — slow rotation' — the promised gesture never executes; the plan's drift action is unrealized.
weak_cycles.suggestion: Either invoke transformElement on the feather (rotation/translate) or call animatePreset('drift') on it — make the stated intent real.

weak_cycles.issue: fadeElement targets 'elem_0006' which corresponds to the cycle-6 warm horizon SVG — correct intent — but the ID assumption is fragile and there's no other gesture to mark the phrase break as composition.
weak_cycles.suggestion: Confirm ID via semantic_label lookup; consider also a subtle paletteShift or holdFrame to make the stillness deliberate.

weak_cycles.issue: Plan called for textAnimate wordByWord on the existing 'what the room held' fragment plus a moonlight breath; instead this re-adds the same text as a new element, creating a duplicate rather than reanimating.
weak_cycles.suggestion: Use textAnimate on the existing cycle-13 text and transformElement(scale) on the moonlight SVG; do not duplicate text content.

weak_cycles.issue: fadeElement targets 'elem_0018' (back-at-window image) but the plan also specifies fading head-bowed, loose linen, and p5 breath by this point — only one element retires when several should.
weak_cycles.suggestion: Issue multiple fades (head-bowed image, loose linen, p5 breath) so the final tableau truly contracts to moonlight + feather + text.

weak_cycles.issue: Fades group_0007 (already retired at 17 attempt) and group_0013, but does not explicitly fade the feather, breath p5, dusk figures, or candle palm — the 'fade everything except moonlight and text' is incomplete and feather contradicts plan (it should remain as part of foreshadow payoff or be explicitly retired).
weak_cycles.suggestion: Per plan ('moonlight on water alone'), fade all remaining images/p5/feather; if the feather is meant to remain, update plan note. Use semantic_label-based fades to be ID-robust.

global_notes: The composition has a strong, coherent arc with thoughtful foreshadow pairs (moonlight 0→13→29, feather 4→27, breath 9→22) and a paradoxical quiet third tahwil that lands well. Palette progression and anticipation offsets are carefully matched to the hijaz/muhayyar trajectory. The main weakness is element-ID hygiene during the descent: several fadeElement calls in cycles 10, 16, 17, 21, 28, 29 appear to guess IDs and either no-op, retire the wrong element, or under-retire — undermining the planned final tableau contraction. Cycles 20 and 22 also drop promised gestures (transformElement on feather; textAnimate wordByWord) in favor of either silence or duplicate placement. Recommend a pass that (a) resolves fades by semantic_label rather than positional IDs, (b) realizes the stated transform/animate gestures rather than skipping or duplicating, and (c) issues bulk retirement at cycle 28–29 so the closing tableau truly reduces to moonlight + one text fragment as the plan promises.
